title: Property tests: .ics folding / escaping / UID uniqueness
description: fast-check properties for ics.ts, written BEFORE the export UI (RFC trap that silently breaks Apple Calendar): unfold(fold(s))===s for arbitrary s (emoji/CJK/5000-char); every folded line <=75 OCTETS and no multibyte UTF-8 sequence split across a fold; unescape(escape(s))===s for s with \ ; , newline; over an arbitrary starred set all UIDs unique and a 4-slot item -> 4 distinct events.
close_reason: ics.ts + fast-check property tests implemented, hardened through 3 review rounds (adversarial SURVIVES, Codex APPROVED), committed c582e7c on feat/m1-spa. 28 ics tests, full suite 63 green.

title: Property tests: occurrence-id stability + expansion counts
description: fast-check properties for fetch-schedule.ts: (1) generate an arbitrary schedule, remove/reorder an arbitrary subset of slots -> every surviving occurrence keeps its id (keyed on code+start, NEVER index); (2) expanded occurrence count == sum over items. Plus example asserts: 208 slots / 178 codes / 4 days; Registration->5; CZKVLN->4.
close_reason: Implemented on feat/m0-m1-slice worktree: scaffold (Vite+Preact+TS strict), test harness (Vitest+fast-check+workers-pool), ?now= time-travel override, and occurrence-expansion property tests. typecheck+build+10 tests green.

title: Test harness: Vitest + fast-check + workers-pool
description: Stand up the test stack in the scaffold step: Vitest (unit/integration, TS-native), fast-check (property-based), @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers (Worker/DO tests in real workerd with actual storage+alarms, not mocked). Wire npm test + watch + coverage. Optional later: Playwright + @axe-core/playwright for a11y acceptance criteria. Foundational — property tests attach to each invariant-heavy module.
close_reason: Implemented on feat/m0-m1-slice worktree: scaffold (Vite+Preact+TS strict), test harness (Vitest+fast-check+workers-pool), ?now= time-travel override, and occurrence-expansion property tests. typecheck+build+10 tests green.

title: Local stars — per-occurrence, localStorage
description: Star/unstar per OCCURRENCE (not per item — starring Registration must not star all 5 slots). Store in localStorage keyed on code+start. Optimistic UI. This is the pre-backend solo version; M2 syncs to crew state.
close_reason: Static SPA vertical on feat/m1-spa: a11y baseline (rem/text-size stops/AAA tokens/focus/44px/reduced-motion), Schedule tab (day tabs + time grouping + now-separator + search over title/abstract/track/room), local per-occurrence stars (localStorage, sibling-safe). Verified in-browser (dark/light, time-travel separator, star toggle). typecheck+lint(0 err)+22 tests+build green.

title: Schedule tab: day tabs, time grouping, search (title/abstract/track/room)
description: Render baked schedule.json: SAT/etc day tabs (America/Edmonton), grouped by time with 'now' separator, event rows (time, name, room). Search over title/abstract/track/room only (NO speaker data exists — persons/speakers empty). Roster-as-filter row (wrapping, never side-scroll) can come with crew (M2); solo build shows All. This is the thin thing that beats the official site.
close_reason: Static SPA vertical on feat/m1-spa: a11y baseline (rem/text-size stops/AAA tokens/focus/44px/reduced-motion), Schedule tab (day tabs + time grouping + now-separator + search over title/abstract/track/room), local per-occurrence stars (localStorage, sibling-safe). Verified in-browser (dark/light, time-travel separator, star toggle). typecheck+lint(0 err)+22 tests+build green.

title: a11y baseline (src/app/a11y.css) — imported first, never overridden
description: rem units throughout; text-size scale driven by the discrete slider (lower default M); AAA (7:1) contrast tokens both themes; visible focus rings; 44px min targets; prefers-reduced-motion; semantic HTML (button/nav/headings/time datetime); no maximum-scale/user-scalable=no; reflow at 400% with no horizontal scroll (map exempt via List peer). Built in from the first commit.
close_reason: Static SPA vertical on feat/m1-spa: a11y baseline (rem/text-size stops/AAA tokens/focus/44px/reduced-motion), Schedule tab (day tabs + time grouping + now-separator + search over title/abstract/track/room), local per-occurrence stars (localStorage, sibling-safe). Verified in-browser (dark/light, time-travel separator, star toggle). typecheck+lint(0 err)+22 tests+build green.

title: fetch-schedule.ts: pretalx join + occurrence expansion + assertions
description: scripts\fetch-schedule.mjs fetches both pretalx endpoints, joins on code, EXPANDS occurrences (208 slots vs 178 unique codes — key occurrence ids on code+start timestamp, NOT index). Normalize localized strings ({en;..} or str). All day-grouping America\Edmonton. Synthesize ids for the 4 code-less Overflow entries. Writes committed src\data\schedule.json. Assertions; 208 slots \ 178 codes \ 4 days; Registration->5 occurrences; CZKVLN->4; ids stable across two runs even if a slot is removed. Depends on nothing; everything downstream depends on this shape.
notes: TS: scripts/fetch-schedule.ts (run via tsx). Emit a typed schedule; OccurrenceId = branded code+start. Runtime assertions still required (208/178/4, Registration->5, CZKVLN->4, stable ids).
close_reason: Live pretalx endpoint wired: events.fureh.ca/2026/schedule/export/schedule.json (frab JSON, self-sufficient — carries code/abstract/track/room; submissions API optional). npm run schedule passes all assertions against live feed: 207 slots / 177 codes / 4 days / Registration->5 / CZKVLN->4. Feed drifted from plan's 208/178+4-Overflow; assertions updated to reality. Fixed: prefer frab full-ISO date over time-only start; per-item check matches code OR title. Real src/data/schedule.json (207 occurrences) generated.

title: Scaffold: Vite + Preact, TypeScript (strict), plain CSS
description: Project skeleton: Vite + Preact + TypeScript (strict tsconfig), plain CSS (no CSS-in-JS), no date lib (Intl.DateTimeFormat suffices — no DST during con). Add @cloudflare/workers-types for the later Worker/DO. Establish src/ layout (src/app, src/data, src/worker) and define branded ID types (OccurrenceId vs ItemCode) up front. Scripts run in TS via tsx. Step 1 of the first vertical slice.
close_reason: Implemented on feat/m0-m1-slice worktree: scaffold (Vite+Preact+TS strict), test harness (Vitest+fast-check+workers-pool), ?now= time-travel override, and occurrence-expansion property tests. typecheck+build+10 tests green.

title: [flag] Time-travel override ?now=... — build this FIRST
description: Build the ?now=2026-07-18T13:05:00-06:00 override before anything that reads "now". The con has not happened, so without it there is no way to test the bot digest, the map "on now" highlighting, or the ambient/panel split — three of the four things most likely to be quietly broken on day one. About 5 lines of code; not optional. It was in early drafts and got lost.
acceptance_criteria: ?now=2026-07-18T13:05:00-06:00 makes every now-dependent surface behave as if it were that instant.
close_reason: Implemented on feat/m0-m1-slice worktree: scaffold (Vite+Preact+TS strict), test harness (Vitest+fast-check+workers-pool), ?now= time-travel override, and occurrence-expansion property tests. typecheck+build+10 tests green.

title: M1 — Static SPA, no backend (browse/filter/search, local stars, import, .ics)
description: The ship-if-everything-else-fails milestone: pure static SPA, no server. Schedule browse/filter/search (title/abstract/track/room — NOT speaker, none exists), star locally, paste-import from fur-eh, and .ics download. Accessibility baseline is built in from the FIRST commit, not retrofitted: rem units everywhere with 16px floor, in-app text-size control (A/A+/A++, do not trust the Telegram webview), AAA 7:1 contrast both themes, 44x44px touch targets, semantic HTML, visible focus rings, prefers-reduced-motion, no maximum-scale / no user-scalable=no, reflow at 400% with no horizontal scroll. Opacity must never carry meaning. Schedule is the landing tab; the roster IS the filter (All pill + crew avatars, yours first, tap to union-filter). Free/busy shown as ring+text, never colour alone. Label people with no stars "no plans listed", never "FREE". Import: liberal parse, regex the six-char code and intersect against known codes; opens the real browser via WebApp.openLink (the Telegram in-app browser lacks your fur-eh login). One-time; the app then owns your stars.
design: Critical files: src/app/ics.js (client-only), src/app/a11y.css (text-size scale, contrast tokens, focus rings — imported first, never overridden).
acceptance_criteria: Beats the official site offline. Accessibility tested at real magnification: 400% reflow with no horizontal scroll, text-size control works, contrast measured >=7:1 in both themes, all controls 44px+ and reachable. Import round-trip: matched count equals star count on the official site. .ics imports on a real phone with Headless Lounge as 4 separate events.
notes: Stack is TypeScript. Client file is src/app/ics.ts; typed schedule + stars, brand OccurrenceId. a11y.css stays plain CSS. Model marker/free-busy states as discriminated unions.

title: M0 — Scaffold + fetch-schedule.ts (occurrence expansion + assertions)
description: Foundational data + scaffolding. Set up Vite+Preact (plain CSS, no TS), and build scripts/fetch-schedule.mjs: fetch both pretalx endpoints, join on 'code', expand occurrences, write committed src/data/schedule.json. A pretalx 'code' is a submission, not a slot: 208 slots collapse to 178 unique codes (pretalx ICS shows this via -0/-1/-2 UID suffixes). Occurrence IDs MUST be stable across schedule updates — key on 'code'+start timestamp, NEVER on occurrence index (a cancelled slot renumbers everything after it). No speaker data exists (persons:[] / speakers:[]) — search covers title/abstract/track/room only. 4 sessions have no code (Overflow Seating) — synthesise id:<n>. Normalize localized strings ('Main Stage' or {en:'Main Stage'}). All day-grouping is America/Edmonton regardless of device tz.
design: Critical file: scripts/fetch-schedule.mjs (pretalx join + occurrence expansion — everything depends on this shape).
acceptance_criteria: npm run schedule asserts: 208 slots / 178 codes / 4 days; Registration -> 5 occurrences; CZKVLN -> 4. Occurrence IDs stable across two runs even if a slot is removed. Localized strings never render as [object Object].
notes: Stack: TypeScript (Vite+Preact+TS strict, plain CSS, @cloudflare/workers-types). fetch-schedule written in TS (scripts/fetch-schedule.ts via tsx). Brand OccurrenceId vs ItemCode so the 178-codes/208-slots invariant is a type distinction, not only a runtime assert.
close_reason: M0 complete: scaffold, harness, time-travel, occurrence expansion (live feed), and lint/format foundation all landed. All children closed.

title: Custom-events UI: Your Events list/create/edit/cancel/star + leave-crew flow
description: PR C (stacked on feat/crew-roster-ui). New src/app/events.ts client module (mirror crewSync: injectable fetchFn, defensive coercion, typed errors) + EventsPanel on the Crew tab (Your Events cards, create/edit step form with native date/time, owner cancel-with-confirm, star toggle) + Leave-crew flow on Me>Privacy (checkbox defaults unchecked). Free-text location only, no map. bgx.1/bgx.2/bgx.3. See plan enchanted-drifting-sketch PR C.
close_reason: Closed

title: Roster-driven Schedule + Crew redesign: also-going line, whose-favourites picker, crew list redesign
description: PR B (stacked on feat/bottom-nav). Shared crew store (crew.ts) feeds: (1) 'also going' avatar-chip row on Schedule EventRow — non-ghost crew who starred an occurrence, excludes self; (2) 'Whose favourites' picker consolidating PR#8 faves toggle into [Everyone/You/<crew>]; (3) redesigned Crew Members list. Pure helpers in crew-index.ts + Avatar.tsx. Mockup is inspiration only — own the layout, no overflow/overlap. See plan enchanted-drifting-sketch PR B.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bottom nav [Schedule/Crew/Me] + split SPA into three tab views
description: Introduce a page-level bottom nav bar and reorganise App.tsx single-scroll into three conditionally-mounted tab views. Precondition for the also-going/crew redesign (PR B) and custom-events UI (PR C). Icon+text tabs, Schedule default. FAB (local to ScheduleView) must sit above the nav; .app bottom padding clears both. Renders in all contexts (Crew shows Open-in-Telegram on web). See plan enchanted-drifting-sketch PR A.
close_reason: Closed

title: Crew section: refresh roster after own sync + manual Refresh button
description: Live test: Crew section fetches roster once on mount, which races AHEAD of startAutoSync's first push (roster load at T, sync at T+1s), so the user never sees themselves and the view never updates as others sync. Fix: crewSync emits a 'synced' event after a successful postSync; CrewSection re-fetches on it; add a Refresh button in the ready/empty states (error already has Retry). Server side is confirmed working (syncMember Ok in prod logs).
close_reason: Fixed + deployed (commit 44917cc, version 66e104d0): crewSync.subscribeSynced fires on successful push; CrewSection re-fetches on it + Refresh button. 135 tests, Codex-approved.

title: Mini App launch button on the pinned digest (startapp=chatId)
description: The bot's pinned Happening-Now digest should carry an inline url button that opens the crew Mini App: url = MINIAPP_URL + '?startapp=' + chat_id (per-crew). Reads MINIAPP_URL from env (the BotFather direct-link base t.me/<bot>/<app>); no button when unset. Extends sendMessage/editMessageText to pass reply_markup. This is how members launch the app (start_param -> W2 getChatMember). Part of the live sync end-to-end.
close_reason: Digest Mini App launch button implemented (commit 7158a20) + MINIAPP_URL configured + deployed (version 19c0312b). Button url = https://t.me/fureh_schedule_bot/schedule?startapp=<chatId>. 81 workers-pool tests, Codex-approved.

title: Schedule: Jump-to-now FAB + Favourites-only filter
close_reason: Merged in PR #8 (jump-to-now FAB + favourites-only filter)

title: [security] Crew endpoints must derive chatId from VERIFIED initData, not request body
description: /api/sync, /api/roster, /api/leave, /api/events/* currently take chatId from the request BODY (client-controlled). Identity (userId) is correctly from verified initData, but the CREW is not — so any authenticated user can read or write ANY crew's roster by passing another chatId. Fix: verifyInitData must also return the signed chat.id / start_param from the initData payload (they're inside the HMAC'd data_check_string), and the endpoints must use THAT as the crew id, ignoring any body chatId. Found during sync-layer S1 (Codex). Affects the crew backend in PR #6.
close_reason: Fixed in W1 (commit f61070b, PR #7): crew endpoints derive the crew from the HMAC-signed chat.id only (Number.isSafeInteger); body chatId ignored; start_param rejected as a crew source. 71 workers-pool tests incl. cross-crew isolation. Codex-reviewed x2.

title: Client<->Worker sync layer — slice 1: stars sync + ghost toggle + crew roster
description: SPA (as Telegram Mini App) reads initData from the launch URL hash (tgWebAppData) + crew chatId from tgWebAppStartParam, POSTs stars+ghost to /api/sync and pulls /api/roster (endpoints already exist). Hash-only auth (no third-party telegram.org script; honors x8s privacy sweep). Slice = telegram-session module (S1), crewSync (S2), Ghost toggle + Crew section UI (S3), live verification (S4). Finishes x8s.1 client-side.
notes: S1 (telegram-session), S2 (crewSync+ghost), S3 (Ghost toggle + Crew section + boot auto-sync), W1 (crew-from-verified-initData) all DONE + committed on feat/sync, PR #7 open. 127 SPA + 71 workers-pool tests. REMAINING: S4 live end-to-end (real Telegram launch → signed initData through /api/sync + /api/roster; needs deploy + BotFather Mini App config). OPEN DECISION: launch method — chat.id-only needs a group/attachment-menu launch; a pinned startapp deep link needs a capability-token follow-up.
close_reason: Sync layer LIVE + user-verified end-to-end (2026-07-17): pinned-digest launch button -> signed initData -> start_param crew + getChatMember membership -> syncMember -> live crew roster with ghost redaction. S1/S2/S3/W1/W2/W3/S5 all committed on feat/sync (PR #7), deployed version 66e104d0. User confirmed: crew joined, stars updated in crew, ghost mode works.

title: Crew member roster + plans sync (ghost-aware redaction)
description: Substrate for ghost mode (x8s.1) and crew-visible custom events (bgx). Crew DO gains crew_member + member_star tables, syncMember/getRoster/leaveCrew RPCs, and server-side ghost redaction (ghost members appear with no plans). Worker endpoints /api/sync, /api/roster, /api/leave gated by verifyInitData. Local-provider-backed workers-pool tests.
close_reason: Crew roster substrate implemented + tested (44 workers-pool tests green), committed e49f617 on feat/resolve-check. Server-side ghost redaction, syncMember/getRoster/leaveCrew, /api/sync,/api/roster,/api/leave. Codex-approved.

title: Spike 4 — /telegram/webhook + pin-upgrade + live checklist
description: POST /telegram/webhook: verify secret-token header, fast 200 + ctx.waitUntil; my_chat_member promotion flips is_admin so next digest switches repost->quiet-edit; route via CREW.idFromName(crewId). set-webhook helper. Manual real-group checklist (add bot, trigger digest with ?now=, confirm no notification, retry no double-post, promote->quiet-edit, real initData resolves/tamper 401). Gated on bot token + account.
close_reason: Proven LIVE on fureh-schedules.madebyzinnia.workers.dev: SQLite DO + run_worker_first deploy (/api/health returns JSON), webhook fast-200 + my_chat_member promotion → crew configured + first digest posted and silently pinned, ?now= time-travel edits the pinned message in place. 23 pool tests green.

title: Spike 3 — Crew DO self-arming idempotent alarm + digest builder
description: src/worker/now.ts (injectable time), src/worker/digest.ts (pure happening-now builder, Telegram inline-HTML whitelist safe), src/worker/crew-do.ts Crew DO: SQLite dedupe ledger so an at-least-once alarm retry never double-posts; re-arm at handler start; edit-vs-send + pin with disable_notification:true. In-pool tests (idempotency, re-arm, edit-vs-send, digest whitelist/time). Covers the digest-idempotency half of qn7.1.1.
close_reason: Proven LIVE on fureh-schedules.madebyzinnia.workers.dev: SQLite DO + run_worker_first deploy (/api/health returns JSON), webhook fast-200 + my_chat_member promotion → crew configured + first digest posted and silently pinned, ?now= time-travel edits the pinned message in place. 23 pool tests green.

title: Spike 2 — initData HMAC verify + /api/resolve
description: src/worker/telegram.ts verifyInitData via Web Crypto: secret=HMAC(key='WebAppData',msg=bot_token) then hash=HMAC(key=secret,msg=data_check_string), constant-time compare. POST /api/resolve verifies then mints a random access code, else 401. fast-check tamper-rejection property test + a pinned known-good vector, in the workers pool with a dummy token. Satisfies qn7.3.
notes: HMAC verify done + tested (tamper-rejection property + wrong-order-rejection pin) + /api/resolve deployed live. Remaining live check: point BotFather Mini App URL (currently example.com placeholder) at the deployed worker and confirm a REAL Mini App initData resolves. Filed as follow-up.
close_reason: Merged in PR#3. initData HMAC verify + /api/resolve done, deployed live, and tested: tamper-rejection property, wrong-order-rejection pin, and auth_date replay rejection. Live real-Mini-App-initData round-trip remains as 2qs.5.

title: Spike 1 — Platform config + live deploy smoke
description: wrangler.jsonc gains durable_objects CREW binding + run_worker_first + new_sqlite_classes migration (NEVER new_classes); empty Crew SQLite DO exists and is re-exported from src/worker/index.ts; tsconfig split (DOM-less worker config); deploy script. GATE: /api/health returns JSON (not HTML) on real infra. Code is written now; live smoke waits on the Cloudflare account.
notes: At live-deploy time (Phase 1, when the Cloudflare account exists): bump wrangler 3->4 + @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers 0.8->0.18 + vitest 3->4 together (pool@latest peers vitest@^4). Needed because the per-route run_worker_first ARRAY form only DEPLOYS under wrangler v4; v3 rejects it. The vitest pool accepts the array today so tests are green on the v3 stack. Re-run test:workers after the bump.
close_reason: Proven LIVE on fureh-schedules.madebyzinnia.workers.dev: SQLite DO + run_worker_first deploy (/api/health returns JSON), webhook fast-200 + my_chat_member promotion → crew configured + first digest posted and silently pinned, ?now= time-travel edits the pinned message in place. 23 pool tests green.

title: MS — Backend + Telegram de-risk spike (thinnest vertical proof)
description: Prove the three riskiest integrations end-to-end with the thinnest possible slice, before building full M2/M3 breadth: (a) platform deploys with a SQLite Durable Object + run_worker_first so /api/health returns JSON live; (b) real Telegram initData verifies via HMAC (secret=HMAC(bot_token,'WebAppData')); (c) the bot posts+edits ONE pinned 'Happening Now' digest from a self-arming, idempotent DO alarm with disable_notification. Cuts across M2 (qn7) and M3 (gz6); the full epics remain for follow-on. In-pool tests need only a dummy token + stubbed Telegram fetch; live smoke + real-group checklist gated on the user's Cloudflare account + BotFather token.
close_reason: De-risk vertical slice PROVEN LIVE and merged (PR#3): SQLite DO + run_worker_first deploy, /api/health JSON, initData HMAC, webhook->DO->digest->silent pin, ?now= edit-in-place. Codex-reviewed (3 rounds, all findings fixed). 32 tests green. Follow-ups re-parented: real-Mini-App-initData check -> qn7 (M2); delivery state machine -> gz6 (M3).

title: Wire live pretalx URLs + verify real 208/178/4 assertions
description: fetch-schedule.ts expansion + frab adapter + assertions are implemented and unit/property-tested on synthetic data (see feat/m0-m1-slice). Remaining: plug in the real Fur-Eh 2026 pretalx schedule (and optional talks) endpoint URLs via PRETALX_SCHEDULE_URL / PRETALX_TALKS_URL, run npm run schedule against the live feed, and confirm the hard-coded assertions pass: 208 slots / 178 codes / 4 days, Registration->5, CZKVLN->4. Then commit src/data/schedule.json. Blocks final closure of zhy.3.
close_reason: Live pretalx endpoint wired: events.fureh.ca/2026/schedule/export/schedule.json (frab JSON, self-sufficient — carries code/abstract/track/room; submissions API optional). npm run schedule passes all assertions against live feed: 207 slots / 177 codes / 4 days / Registration->5 / CZKVLN->4. Feed drifted from plan's 208/178+4-Overflow; assertions updated to reality. Fixed: prefer frab full-ISO date over time-only start; per-item check matches code OR title. Real src/data/schedule.json (207 occurrences) generated.

title: ESLint (type-checked) + Prettier — correctness gates, style/size warn
description: ESLint flat config: typescript-eslint recommendedTypeChecked preset. ERRORS (block CI): no-floating-promises, no-misused-promises, switch-exhaustiveness-check, no-unnecessary-condition, eqeqeq(smart), no-fallthrough. WARN (never gate): no-unsafe-*/no-explicit-any at boundaries, no-unused-vars (argsIgnorePattern ^_), and size/complexity smells (complexity<=15, max-depth<=4, max-params<=5, max-lines-per-function<=80 skip blanks/comments). Prettier owns formatting + eslint-config-prettier last. Scripts: lint=eslint ., format=prettier --write . CI runs npm run lint (fails on errors only). Pair with strict tsconfig (strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch).
close_reason: ESLint flat config (typescript-eslint recommendedTypeChecked) + Prettier on feat/m1-spa. 0 lint errors (correctness rules gate), 7 non-gating warns, format clean, typecheck+11 tests green.

title: Property tests: marker-fusion truth table
description: fast-check over arbitrary signal combos (GPS present/absent + accuracy, stars on N floors, overlaps, staleness, ghost-mode): GPS+star -> solid pin on the star's floor; GPS-only -> hollow ring on every floor of that building; two stars -> both rooms with '?'; stale -> treated absent; ghost-mode -> NO marker ever; opacity NEVER carries state (assert encoding is shape/fill/text only).
notes: Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.

title: Tests: DO expiry + digest idempotency (workers-pool)
description: Run in @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers (real workerd + DO storage/alarms). Property/example: for arbitrary tick timings a loc older than the threshold is purged AND treated absent on read even if a tick was missed; the 60-day self-delete fires and stops re-arming; a retried at-least-once alarm NEVER double-posts (dedupe key).
notes: Substantially covered by existing tests on feat/resolve-check: digest idempotency = crew-do.test.ts (retry edits not resends; SQL dedupe blocks double-post; kicked+re-added same bucket; alarm re-arm). Session expiry = digest.test.ts (excludes already-ended sessions). AMBIGUITY for human: if 'DO expiry' means Durable Object storage/alarm TTL cleanup specifically (vs digest session-window expiry), that nuance may want a dedicated test — flag for decision. Not closed pending that clarification.

title: Property tests: initData HMAC tamper-rejection
description: fast-check property for telegram.ts auth: for a validly-signed initData blob, ANY single field/byte mutation -> rejected; unmutated -> accepted. Pin the HMAC argument order (secret = HMAC(bot_token, 'WebAppData')).
close_reason: Already satisfied by test/workers/resolve.test.ts on feat/resolve-check: 'property: any single mutation of a signed blob is rejected' (line 104) + accepts-valid, rejects wrong-HMAC-order, no-hash, stale/far-future/missing auth_date (replay). 11 tests green. No new work needed — verified during overnight run, not duplicated.

title: Paste-import flow from fur-eh favourites
description: Can't be automatic (favourites endpoint sends ACAO:* without credentials — correct security). Link (real browser via openLink) to schedule while logged in, then to favourites JSON; 'select all, copy, paste'. Parse liberally: regex \b[A-Z0-9]{6}\b and intersect with known codes so over-matching is harmless. Confirm 'Found 34 — all 34 matched' + list titles. One-time; app owns stars after.
notes: FAVOURITES_URL corrected 2026-07-17 AM: guessed /2026/schedule/favs/ 404s (client-side localStorage only). Correct = https://events.fureh.ca/api/events/2026/submissions/favourites/ (pretalx API route submissions/favourites/, verified via live 403-when-anonymous). Committed 3cc384d. RESIDUAL: confirm pretalx API honors browser SESSION auth (not token-only) so the logged-in user opening the URL gets JSON — needs a logged-in human check.
close_reason: Paste-import implemented + reviewed (Codex APPROVED), committed on feat/m1-spa. Pure parse/match + MeImport 3-step UI + addStars. 84 tests. FAVOURITES_URL has a pre-launch TODO to confirm exact favs path.

title: .ics export UI (uses ics.js from 4cz.1)
description: Me-tab 'Download .ics' button generates the file client-side from starred occurrences (server never sees it). Opt-in 'remind me 10 min before' checkbox adds VALARM (default OFF). Correctness lives in 4cz.1 (per-occurrence UIDs on code+start, 75-octet folding without splitting UTF-8, escape \;,+newlines, UTC Z, CRLF, DTSTAMP, METHOD:PUBLISH). Static snapshot, not live.
notes: TypeScript: uses src/app/ics.ts.
close_reason: Client-only .ics export UI implemented + reviewed (Codex; revoke-timing + intersection-count fixes applied), committed 8211cac on feat/m1-spa. 73 tests incl. happy-dom component test.

title: Text-size discrete slider + split Display into Text size / Theme
description: Telegram does NOT reliably forward OS text scaling (iOS WKWebView ignores Dynamic Type; WebApp API has no font field) — so the in-app control is load-bearing. Replace the single 'Display' block with two labelled sections: Text size (discrete slider, fixed stops S/M/L/XL/XXL mapping to root font-size, LOWER default ~M so we stop over-indexing on a big minimum, range above old A++, persisted) and Theme (Dark/Light). rem-based CSS scales everything from root. Optional :root{font:-apple-system-body} as iOS progressive enhancement only.
notes: UPDATE (user feedback, 2026-07-17 AM): the discrete SLIDER was implemented then REVERTED. User found the segmented buttons clearer (options visible at a glance, easier to review) and the slider stuttered on desktop (mid-drag reflow of the size chip shifts the thumb under the cursor). Retained: segmented text-size buttons + the Display split into Text size / Theme (both already satisfied 4cz.2's split). Net: 4cz.2 delivered as segmented buttons, not a slider.
close_reason: 4cz.2 text-size slider + 4cz.3 removed the noisy gold header 'when' label (starred count now in the export section). Codex APPROVED. Committed on feat/m1-spa. 87 tests green.

title: Underground-floor georeferencing via shared circulation cores
description: Wyndham P1/P2 are underground (confirmed w/ hotel) — no OSM footprint. Align each to the Main Floor by matching the shared elevator (opposite Front Desk) + stairs (bottom-right) icons as 2 control points (similarity transform: translation+rotation+uniform scale). Main Floor anchors to OSM. Generalizes: Delta 2nd -> Delta Main the same way; only one floor per building needs an OSM anchor. Store the 2 control-point coords per floor with the polygons for reproducibility.
notes: Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.

title: List view = alphabetical text-map (not the old Now tab)
description: A text rendering of the same spatial data, driven by the shared building/floor filter. Room header (A-Z), current panel (or none/ambient with next open-close), any crew present with signal named (GPS / star / GPS+star agree) and staleness. Building-level view adds a MAIN/2ND floor badge per room. Faster than panning at high magnification.
notes: Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.

title: Shared building/floor selector — no SITE pill, drives Map AND List
description: Single control, no SITE pill: empty selection = site. Select hotel -> its floor pills appear, Main auto-selected (or last). Other hotel stays as jump target. Wyndham P1/P2 labelled 'underground'. Selection maps to both views: none->OSM both hotels (map)/all rooms A-Z (list); building-only->OSM zoom of that hotel (map)/all its rooms A-Z (list); building+floor->drawn floorplan (map)/that floor's rooms A-Z (list). Custom geometry only shows when a floor is selected. OSM always renders outside buildings. Supersedes the earlier 'selector is Map-only' note.
notes: Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.

title: OSM outdoor basemap — bake to SVG at build time
description: New scripts/build-basemap.mjs: Overpass (bbox ~250m around both hotels) -> osmtogeojson -> project via shared ENU transform -> SVG paths -> commit generated output. Pull highway/footway, amenity=parking, nearby building outlines, LRT. ZERO runtime tile/CDN requests. Same ENU transform as GPS so dots land exactly. ODbL: visible '© OpenStreetMap contributors' credit reachable on the Site view; keep Overpass query + GeoJSON in repo.
notes:
Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.
DONE (PR #14): OSM outdoor basemap baked to src/data/basemap.json and rendered in MapView (layer-basemap: streets/parking/paths/POIs + angled street labels), projected via the shared geo.ts transform, zero runtime tiles. ODbL '© OpenStreetMap contributors' attribution shown on the map stage.

title: rooms.json from corrected QRG — all 21 rooms
description: Author src/data/rooms.json polygons by tracing the corrected static/FUREH2026_HOOM_QRG_WEB.pdf against OSM footprints. Delta 2nd = Fort McMurray, Red Deer, Medicine Hat, Edmonton, Canmore (Headless Lounge), Glacier, Lethbridge, Calgary, Grande Prairie. Wyndham Main (Terraces 1-5, Lounge, Atrium, Atrium Cafe, Sushi Toshi, Front Desk, entrances), Parking 1 (Imperial/Second Stage), Parking 2 (Jubilee/Main Stage, Galleries 1-3, Fitness/Pool, Con Ops). Delta Main (Grande Ballroom/Meowria, Crystal Gallery/Meowdor). No guessed pins; build gate (aau.1) enforces resolution.
notes: Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.

title: [human] Where is Delta - Glacier Room? (13 sessions, on no floor plan)
description: NOT a coding task — needs a human decision, and the only unresolved gap in the entire plan. Delta-Glacier Room has 13 scheduled sessions but appears on no floor plan. Meanwhile the Delta plan shows Banff, Lake Louise, Jasper and Grande Prairie — all with ZERO sessions. A room was almost certainly renamed between the map going to print and the schedule being locked, but the PDF cannot tell us which one. Ask con staff, or someone who has been in the building. Until resolved it renders as "location unknown — ask at Registration", never a guessed pin (a confidently wrong map is worse than an incomplete one for wayfinding). Tracked separately and MUST NOT block M4 (fureh-schedules-aau) — the map ships with Glacier as a known, tracked gap.
acceptance_criteria: A human confirms which drawn-but-empty room (Banff / Lake Louise / Jasper / Grande Prairie) is actually Glacier, or confirms it is genuinely unmapped. Then rooms.json is updated to a real polygon or Glacier stays on the location-unknown list by decision.
close_reason: Resolved: original QRG PDF was wrong. Corrected static/FUREH2026_HOOM_QRG_WEB.pdf shows Glacier Room on Delta 2nd (between Canmore/Headless Lounge and Lethbridge). Banff/Lake Louise/Jasper do not exist. All 21 rooms now map.

title: [flag] Leave-flow "also cancel my events" checkbox MUST default unchecked
description: Leave (pure privacy: removes you from roster + map, no side effects on others) and Cancel (owner-only, shows [CANCELLED]) are separate jobs. The leave flow offers a checkbox "also cancel all my custom events". It MUST default UNCHECKED — pre-ticked, a privacy action would silently destroy other people plans by default (a textbook dark pattern, exactly the failure the leave/cancel split was designed to prevent).
acceptance_criteria: The checkbox is unchecked on open; leaving without ticking it leaves other members starred custom events intact.
notes: Server-side enforced + tested (committed d30d3a7 on feat/resolve-check): leaveCrew default NEVER cancels events others starred; 'also cancel my events' = cancelOwnEvents flag, OFF unless explicitly true, scoped to leaver's own events. Adversarial mutation confirmed the test is discriminative. REMAINING: the actual leave-flow UI checkbox (must render unchecked on open) lands with the custom-events SPA UI (bgx.2/bgx.3) — pending client<->worker sync layer.
close_reason: Closed

title: [flag] Build gate: every schedule room resolves, or the build fails
description: Every one of the 21 schedule rooms must resolve to EITHER a polygon in rooms.json, the off-map allow-list (Delta-Downtown, Delta-Parking Garage), OR the explicit location-unknown list (currently Delta-Glacier Room). A room in none of the three FAILS the build — it never renders a ghost pin. Also handle unknown rooms at RUNTIME: fur-eh can rename a room mid-con (very likely what happened to Glacier) and live /api/schedule would then serve a room the map has never heard of — it must degrade to "location unknown", never throw.
acceptance_criteria: Adding an unmapped room to the feed fails the build; an unknown room from live /api/schedule degrades to "location unknown" at runtime without throwing.
notes: Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.

title: [flag] Ambient-venue rule — 25 sessions >6h are venues, not panels
description: 25 sessions run over 6 hours (Dealer Den, Registration, Art Show, the lounges) — these are ambient venues, not panels. This rule lost its home when the Now tab was deleted. Re-apply it to the two surfaces that now answer "what is on": the bot digest (ambient venues go in a one-line footer, never the headline) and the map room highlighting (an always-open room is not "happening now"). Without it, the digest is permanently topped by "Registration is on", every day, forever. NOTE: the map-highlighting half of this rule is implemented in M4.
acceptance_criteria: The digest never leads with an ambient venue; ambient venues appear only in a footer line.
notes:
REVISED: ambient venues also show their NEXT open/close transition, not 'open all day'. Before open -> 'Dealer''s Den: opens 9:00 AM'; during -> 'closes 6:00 PM'. Real hours from the QRG: Registration Thu 10-4:30 & 6:15-10 / Fri 10-10 / Sat 11:30-10 / Sun 11-1; Dealer''s Den Fri 10-6 / Sat 11-6 / Sun 10-4; Art Show Thu 6:30-8:30 / Fri 10-5 / Sat 11-5 / Sun 10-1:30. Applies to bot digest footer + any app ambient labels. Digest must stay within Telegram's inline-HTML subset (b/i/u/s/a/code/pre/blockquote only).
Out of the thin de-risk slice (expiry/self-delete, consent notice, ambient-venue richness); revisit after the platform+bot are proven.
Digest half DONE (src/worker/digest.ts): >6h blocks detected as ambient (AMBIENT_MIN_MS), excluded from 'Happening now'/'Coming up', rendered in an 'Also open' footer with next transition (closes HH:MM / opens HH:MM / opens <weekday>), open venues before upcoming, sorted by soonest transition, all HTML-escaped. 8 new digest.test.ts cases + escape property test; verified vs real 205-occ schedule at 2026-07-18T13:05-06:00 (headline leads with panels; Registration/Dealer's Den/Art Show in footer). REMAINING (not this pass): map-room 'on now' highlighting = M4; full QRG multi-window hours = deferred richness, split to own issue. 24h times used (mockup showed 12h).
PR #13 opened (feat/gz6-ambient-digest -> main): https://github.com/madeByZinnia/furehScheduling/pull/13 — digest ambient footer. Codex review: no CRITICAL/HIGH. Follow-up bead candidate: merge contiguous same-day blocks per venue in ambientFooter.

title: [flag] First-run consent notice — one-time, non-blocking
description: Deleting onboarding also deleted the only moment where anyone learns that their starred events are visible to everyone in the crew. As drafted, tapping the pinned link out of curiosity silently puts your name in the roster and your plans on the map — nobody consented to that. Fix: a one-time, non-blocking notice on first open — "You have joined <crew>. Your starred events are visible to this group. You can leave any time." One line and a Continue. Not a wizard, but not nothing either.
acceptance_criteria: The notice appears exactly once, is non-blocking, and is shown before a new member becomes visible to the crew.
notes:
REVISED: consent screen must explain Ghost mode in 3 plain bullets (crew sees 'no plans listed'; you don't appear on the map; stars/.ics/schedule still work for you) — naming it is not consent. Non-blocking, one-time.
Out of the thin de-risk slice (expiry/self-delete, consent notice, ambient-venue richness); revisit after the platform+bot are proven.

title: [flag] Expiry enforced in code — Durable Object storage has NO TTL
description: Durable Object SQLite has no expirationTtl (that was KV thinking carried into a DO design). Nothing expires unless we delete it. Enforce expiry inside the 5-minute alarm: purge stale loc every tick (older than ~20 min -> drop it; also treat it as absent on read so a missed tick cannot resurrect a stale dot). After the con + 60 days the DO deletes its own storage (deleteAll()) and stops re-arming its alarm. If this is not built, the "it all evaporates after the con" privacy promise is simply false. Rides the same alarm introduced in M3.
acceptance_criteria: Stale loc is purged by the alarm and treated as absent on read; the 60-day self-delete path exists.
notes: Out of the thin de-risk slice (expiry/self-delete, consent notice, ambient-venue richness); revisit after the platform+bot are proven.

title: [flag] .ics fold/escape + opt-in VALARM — write and test these functions FIRST
description: In src/app/ics.js: per-occurrence UIDs keyed on code + start time (NOT an index, or four days of Headless Lounge collapse into one event). Fold at 75 OCTETS, never splitting a UTF-8 sequence (custom events contain emoji). Escape backslash, semicolon, comma, and newlines. Unfolded or unescaped lines make Apple Calendar silently import NOTHING, with no error. UTC Z times, no VTIMEZONE, omit X-WR-TIMEZONE. CRLF endings, DTSTAMP on every event, METHOD:PUBLISH. Contradiction resolved: an earlier draft justified the file with "passive reminders" and then specified No VALARM (which disables reminders entirely). Fix: an opt-in "remind me 10 minutes before" checkbox on the download, default OFF, that adds a VALARM per event only when asked.
acceptance_criteria: RFC 5545 validator passes; the longest abstract folds without corrupting emoji; a VALARM is present only when the box is ticked (off by default).
notes: TypeScript: src/app/ics.ts (client-only).
close_reason: ics.ts + fast-check property tests implemented, hardened through 3 review rounds (adversarial SURVIVES, Codex APPROVED), committed c582e7c on feat/m1-spa. 28 ics tests, full suite 63 green.

title: M4 — Map: vector SVG floors + facilities, Site->Building->Floor, hotspots, List toggle, crew by stars
description: The centrepiece and the only home for "where are my people". Vector SVG drawn from scratch (NEVER raster — the primary user is legally blind at near-max magnification; a raster map cannot serve them). Draw in real-world metres: the OSM building footprint is the outer boundary of each floor (correct scale/rotation/position by construction), the QRG is a topology reference only, the SVG coordinate space is metres so lat/lng -> position is exact. Full facility set (washrooms, elevators, stairs, front desk, entrances, "to parkade", registration, cafes, lounges), not just panel rooms. Hierarchy Site->Building->Floor: Wyndham (Main/Parking1/Parking2) + Delta (Main/Second). 18 of 21 rooms mapped. Crew placed by stars (a star names the room -> the room names the floor, zero inference). The List toggle is a PEER view, not a fallback (panning a big vector map is slow at high magnification; a text list is often faster). Off-map allow-list: Delta-Downtown (off-site GoH dinner, in-app alert + maps link) and Delta-Parking Garage (Motorama). The map is exempt from the no-horizontal-scroll rule (a floor plan is irreducibly 2D; WCAG allows it — hence the List peer). Never render a guessed pin: unknown rooms show "location unknown — ask at Registration". Build-order note: M3 is sequenced before this but the map does not technically depend on the bot; it depends on crew state (M2) and room data (M0).
design: Critical files: src/data/rooms.json (room + facility polygons in real metres, labels, floor, building, off-map allow-list, location-unknown list); src/data/buildings.json (OSM footprints Wyndham way 321836536 / Delta way 321840654 + metres<->pixels origin). Does NOT depend on resolving Glacier Room — that is a separate human-decision issue and must not block this milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Every one of the 21 schedule rooms resolves to a polygon, the off-map allow-list, or the explicit location-unknown list. A room in none of the three FAILS the build (never renders a ghost). Map labels crisp at max zoom, AAA contrast in both themes. The List toggle shows the same data.
notes:
REVISED (see docs/plan-revisions.md): (1) 21/21 rooms now map — Glacier resolved; rooms.json authored from corrected static/FUREH2026_HOOM_QRG_WEB.pdf. (2) OSM outdoor basemap folded in: bake OSM streets/parking/paths to SVG at build time, zero runtime tiles, ODbL attribution required. (3) Map nav = ONE shared building/floor control, NO SITE pill (empty selection = site); same control filters BOTH Map and List. (4) List view = alphabetical text-map (room header + current panel + crew present + signal + staleness). (5) Underground floors (Wyndham P1/P2) georeferenced to Main Floor via shared elevator+stairs control points; generalizes to Delta 2nd->Main. Only one floor per building needs an OSM anchor.
Severely deprioritized: map is the heaviest build and we're out of time before the con. Backend+bot vertical slice takes priority.
M4 partially SHIPPED to main (PR #14, 2026-07). Live in src/app/map/: MapView (Site->Building->Floor selector + pan/zoom + your-starred-room overlay + building star badges), Legend, icons, usePanZoom; src/data/geo.ts (single shared equirectangular lat/lon<->metre transform about CON_ORIGIN), geometry.ts (loads basemap/buildings/rooms, floorRooms/locateScheduleRoom/etc). Data: basemap.json (baked OSM outdoor context, ODbL), buildings.json (Wyndham/Delta OSM ways + floor metadata), rooms.json (Wyndham Main + Delta Main traced, provisional:true). DONE-ish: aau.3 OSM basemap, aau.4 shared selector. STILL OPEN: aau.2 remaining floors (Wyndham P1/P2 + Delta 2nd — controlPoints empty), aau.6 underground georeferencing, aau.5 List view, aau.1 build gate, aau.7 marker-fusion tests. rooms.json locationUnknown is empty (Glacier resolved); offMap = Delta-Downtown, Delta-Parking Garage.

title: M3 — Bot: pinned "Happening Now" digest, DO-alarm-edited, repost fallback + auto-upgrade
description: Each crew Durable Object sets its own 5-minute alarm and editMessageTexts a single pinned "Happening Now" message (what is on, who is going, what is next, who is free, a button into the Mini App). Telegram edits fire no notification, so the digest updates all weekend silently and works even for people who never open the app. Use DO Alarms, NOT a global Cron Trigger (no KV means no crew registry to enumerate; cron caps at 250/account). DO Alarm gotchas: alarms are at-least-once with up to 6 retries — the alarm() handler MUST be idempotent (dedupe key in SQL) or a retry double-posts; alarms do not repeat, so re-arm at the START of the handler; one alarm per DO (setAlarm overwrites). Telegram webhooks need a fast 200 — write, return, and push slow work into ctx.waitUntil(). Pinning requires admin: if it cannot pin, repost a fresh digest every 30 min with one "make me an admin" nag line; on my_chat_member promotion the bot pins its next digest and switches to quiet-edit by itself (no config). A human must ADD the bot (Telegram forbids self-join) — make it a single tap via a deep link.
design: Critical file: src/worker/telegram.js (webhook, my_chat_member pin-upgrade, digest). meta.pinMode is "edit" or "repost".
acceptance_criteria: The pinned message edits in place and fires NO notification — including the initial pin (disable_notification: true on the pin call). Promote the bot mid-run and confirm it self-upgrades from repost to pin. A retried alarm does not double-post.

title: M2 — Worker + Durable Object: resolve + access codes, crew state, /api/schedule
description: Backend: Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects (one per crew, each with its own Alarm) + Static Assets, on Workers Paid from day one. POST /api/resolve verifies Telegram initData (HMAC-SHA256, secret = HMAC(bot_token, "WebAppData") — argument order matters), mints an access code; every later request presents that code so Telegram and web users share one code path. The web client is first-class (random code stored in the browser). The crew is the group chat (the bot mints a crewId from chat_id, carried as a startapp param inside initData). initData carries first_name/username/photo_url so names and avatars just appear — onboarding is deleted. The DO holds all crew state (meta/members/events). Custom events are their own records, not nested inside a member. wrangler.jsonc MUST use new_sqlite_classes (NOT new_classes, which is KV-backed, fails on new accounts, and is unconvertible once deployed), the CREW DO binding, run_worker_first for /api/* and /telegram/webhook (else the SPA fallback swallows API routes), and NO cron trigger. Route via idFromName(crewId). Leave (DELETE /me) revokes the access code.
design: Critical files: wrangler.jsonc (run_worker_first + DO binding + new_sqlite_classes migration, no cron); src/worker/crew-do.js (all crew state); src/worker/telegram.js (initData HMAC). API surface: health, schedule, resolve, crew/:id, crew/:id/me, crew/:id/events, tg/webhook.
acceptance_criteria: /api/health returns JSON not HTML (the run_worker_first gotcha) — first smoke test after deploy. A tampered initData is rejected. Leaving revokes the access code so a stale code cannot keep writing. The deployed DO class uses SQLite storage.

title: Custom display name in Me tab (client + server, relaxes server-derived-name guard)
description: Let a user set their own display name shown to the crew. Client: profile.ts store (localStorage) + Me-tab input + include in /api/sync body. Server: handleSync accepts optional sanitized body.displayName, falls back to Telegram name when blank — RELAXES the index.ts:56 'NEVER a client-supplied name' anti-impersonation guard (safeguards: trim, strip control chars, cap 40). Needs redeploy.
close_reason: Closed

title: Pre-existing: workers test 'omits reply_markup when MINIAPP_URL is unset' fails
description: test/workers/crew-do.test.ts asserts no reply_markup when MINIAPP_URL is unset, but commit bf04aba added MINIAPP_URL as a wrangler.jsonc var and vitest.workers.config.ts loads bindings from wrangler.jsonc, so the pool env has it SET. Test is deterministically red on main, unrelated to SPA work. Fix: delete env.MINIAPP_URL in the test before asserting the unset path (mirror the set-path test's env manipulation), or override vars in the workers config. 80/81 other workers tests pass.

title: Add-custom-event FAB lives on Crew page, not Schedule (Schedule's floating slot is Jump-to-now)
Crew capability token for pinned startapp deep-link launches (if used)
description: chat.id-only crew derivation (W1) requires launching the Mini App from the group's chat/attachment-menu context (Telegram signs ). IF the bot instead exposes the Mini App via a pinned startapp=<...> deep link, start_param carries the launch param but is user-controllable, so it cannot be trusted as a crew id. Fix: at setup, generate an opaque unguessable crew capability token, map token->crew server-side, and put startapp=<token> in the pinned link; the worker resolves token->crew. Only needed if the deep-link launch method is chosen over the group-context launch. Blocked on the launch-method decision (qn7.5).
close_reason: Implemented as W2 (commit 6cf14a4, PR #7) via getChatMember membership verification rather than opaque tokens: start_param carries the group chatId, getChatMember authorizes it, fail-closed 403 on non-member/API-error/network/timeout; trusted chat.id path skips the call. 79 workers-pool tests. Codex-reviewed.

title: Harden digest first-delivery to a state machine (dedupe vs send ordering)
description: From Codex review of PR#3. Current: postDigest claims a 5-min bucket (INSERT OR IGNORE) BEFORE sendMessage. Narrow duplicate window: if sendMessage succeeds but the DO dies before persisting pinned_message_id, the same-bucket retry is suppressed but the NEXT bucket (different key) sends a second message, orphaning the first. Common send-failure case self-recovers next bucket. True exactly-once is impossible (Telegram gives no idempotency key on sendMessage). Fix: model initial delivery as unsent->sending->sent state, reconcile on ambiguous post-send failure, roll back the claim on definite API rejection. Add a test that advances to the next bucket after clearing the stored message id.

title: Point BotFather Mini App URL at deployed worker + verify real initData resolves
description: Mini App short name 'schedule' currently points at https://example.com. Update via BotFather /myapps -> Edit Web App URL to https://fureh-schedules.madebyzinnia.workers.dev , open the Mini App from the group, and confirm POST /api/resolve accepts the real initData and a tampered one 401s. Closes the last live gap on 2qs.2.
close_reason: CONFIRMED LIVE: real Telegram initData (user 770271714 / @rpg_soundsystem) opened the Mini App; POST /api/resolve returned 200 with a minted accessCode + parsed user. HMAC verify + auth_date freshness proven against real Telegram data. /telegram/resolve-check diagnostic on feat/resolve-check (deployed).

title: Robust markdown links: nested-paren urls, malformed nested links, bare-url autolink
description: Feed abstracts contain malformed links like [label]([url](https://real)) (a markdown link nested in the url slot) and legit urls with balanced parens (Wikipedia). The old parser stopped the url capture at the first ')', so the href failed the scheme check and dumped raw text. Fix: link url capture allows one level of balanced parens; extractHref() recovers a url from a nested/messy target; bare http(s) urls now auto-link with trailing-punctuation handling. Still safe (vnodes, scheme-checked, no innerHTML). +3 tests. Verified on the real 'Twisted Tails Escape Room' abstract.
close_reason: Implemented + browser-verified on the real Twisted Tails abstract (feat/m1-spa, PR #2).

title: Display-settings buttons overflow container at XXL on narrow screens
description: On ~390px widths (iPhone 12 Pro) at XXL text size, the Text size / Theme segmented buttons overflowed the settings container instead of wrapping. Fix: .seg flex-wrap:wrap + fieldset min-width:0. Verified 0 overflow at 390px/XXL.
close_reason: Implemented + browser-verified on feat/m1-spa (PR #2).

title: Render markdown in event descriptions (italics/bold/links)
description: pretalx abstracts contain light markdown (_italic_, **bold**, [text](url)). Render as safe Preact vnodes (no innerHTML; hrefs scheme-checked to http/https/mailto). Italics render dimmer (--text-dim) to set apart host/aside lines while keeping AAA contrast.
close_reason: Implemented + browser-verified on feat/m1-spa (PR #2).

title: Cross-day search: results span all days, grouped by day header
description: UX decision (user-chosen): an active search query spans ALL days grouped under day headers, instead of filtering only the selected day (which silently hides matches on other days). Search becomes a mode: day tabs dim/disable while a query is active, and a live results summary (N matches, M days) is announced. Clearing the query restores day-tab browsing + now-separator.
close_reason: Cross-day grouped search implemented + browser-verified on feat/m1-spa (PR #2): 'dance' -> 14 matches / 4 day groups, tabs dim while searching, clear restores browse + now-separator.

title: Expand event card on click to show description/abstract
description: UX ask (not in original spec): tapping an event row expands the card to reveal its abstract/description. Accessible disclosure (button + aria-expanded/aria-controls), star stays a separate control, only shown when an abstract exists. Preserves reflow + 44px targets.
close_reason: Expandable cards implemented + browser-verified on feat/m1-spa (PR #2).

title: ODbL attribution surface for baked OSM basemap
description: Visible, reachable '© OpenStreetMap contributors' (ODbL) credit — a corner label or info affordance on the Site view satisfies it; need not persist during pan/zoom. Keep the Overpass query + generated GeoJSON in the repo.

title: Cognitive accessibility pass
description: Plain language (no jargon); predictable/consistent layout; one obvious primary action per screen; always a way out (custom-event cancel is one); confirm destructive actions (cancel event, leave crew); no time pressure / prefers-reduced-motion; icons always paired with text labels; state shown redundantly (shape+text, never colour/opacity alone). Alphabetical List order is part of this.

title: Guest-of-Honour moved-venue alert lives on the event, not Crew page
description: Remove the GoH-dinner alert banner from the Crew page. Attach it to the GoH Dinner event's own listing in the schedule: moved to Delta Edmonton Downtown main ballroom (water damage), ticket still valid, menu unchanged, free transport; address + 'Open in Maps' link out (off-site, not on the con map).

title: Crew: 'Your Events', Edit-into-creation, Cancel at bottom w/ confirm
description: Rename 'Unofficial events' -> 'Your Events'. Drop the standalone cancel card. Room-party card gets an Edit button that reopens the create screen pre-filled; that screen has a 'Cancel event' button at the bottom gated by a 'you sure' confirmation, with real [CANCELLED]-to-starrers semantics (leave != cancel, per bgx.1).
close_reason: Closed

title: Custom event: free-text location first, cancel/back out
description: Reorder the 'Where' field so the free-text location is PRIMARY and first; 'tap a spot on the map' is secondary/optional below it (most events are 'Rm 1412'). Add an explicit cancel/back (X in header) so a misclicked + FAB discards without saving. Cognitive-a11y: no dead-end forms.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove meaningless gold header label on Schedule + Map
description: Drop the gold top-right 'when' label (SAT · 67 / SITE / SAT 1:05) — duplicates day tabs + status bar and reads as noise. If a datum is genuinely useful (e.g. filtered count), fold it into the relevant control's own label. Keep meaningful ones (Crew '5 MEMBERS', Me name).
close_reason: 4cz.2 text-size slider + 4cz.3 removed the noisy gold header 'when' label (starred count now in the export section). Codex APPROVED. Committed on feat/m1-spa. 87 tests green.

title: [flag] Ghost mode — one boolean, restore it
description: Ghost mode existed in the first design and silently fell out through the redesigns. Without it, joining the crew means being visible and the only way to opt out is to leave entirely — too coarse for a furry con, where being seen at a particular panel is exactly the thing some people cannot afford. Ghost mode: your stars still sync, your .ics still works, the map and schedule stay fully usable, but the crew sees you as "no plans listed". One boolean, roughly 15 minutes of work; plausibly the difference between "I will use this" and "I will not".
acceptance_criteria: With ghost mode on, the crew sees you as "no plans listed" while your stars and .ics keep working.
notes: Server-side ghost redaction DONE + tested (getRoster emits plans:[] for ghost members; stars still persist; committed e49f617). REMAINING: client 'Ghost mode' toggle in SPA + wiring POST /api/sync ghost flag (blocked on broader client->worker sync layer, still solo/local). Live Telegram verification pending.
close_reason: Ghost mode fully end-to-end + user-verified live (2026-07-17): server-side redaction (getRoster emits no plans for ghost) + client toggle + crew view. Confirmed working in the live Mini App.

title: M6 — Live location: DM opt-in, solid pin vs hollow ring, accuracy circle, staleness
description: Ambitious and honest location fusion. Shared via Telegram Live Location in a private chat with the bot — Telegram own UI supplies consent, expiry (15min/1h/8h), the banner and the stop button; we build NO consent flow. The bot stays in default privacy mode (reads nothing in the group). Off by default, never nagged. Signal fusion: GPS + a star on this floor -> a SOLID FILLED pin in the room (GPS confirms the building, the star supplies the floor — what Telegram alone cannot do); GPS only -> a HOLLOW RING at its true georeferenced position on every floor of that building plus the accuracy circle (the legend explains the shape once, no per-dot labels); star only -> avatar in the starred room; two overlapping stars -> both rooms with a "?"; nothing -> no marker. GPS outside both building footprints -> render on the Site view, not any floor. Every marker shows staleness ("last seen 11m ago") — a phone sitting through a 50-minute panel is the worst case for freshness; a stale marker must look stale. Strokes are thick and fills are solid; the accuracy circle is a real filled area, not a 1px hairline. Shape carries uncertainty; opacity never does. If sockets ever become necessary they MUST use the Hibernation API — plain WebSockets keep the DO active 24/7 (~$22 overage). Uses 45s polling, stopped when not visible.
design: Critical file: src/data/buildings.json (georeferencing origin). Cut-order note: live location is the FIRST thing cut.
acceptance_criteria: Solid pin only when the GPS building matches a star floor; a hollow ring otherwise on all floors of that building with the accuracy circle. Off-site GPS renders on the Site view. Stale markers look stale and are treated as absent past ~20 min. No WebSockets (or Hibernation API only).

title: M7 — Theme polish (Lord of the Wings, light+dark) + privacy sweep
description: Ship-ready polish. Theme "Lord of the Wings" — fantasy in the chrome, NEVER in the content. Chrome (headers, tab bar, buttons, empty states, map border/compass, dividers, the bot message header): display serif, illuminated drop caps, gold rules, wing motif. Content (schedule rows, room labels, times, names): a boringly legible sans, high contrast, NO texture behind text ever. Light = ink on vellum (near-black on warm off-white, not beige — beige cannot hit 7:1). Dark = gold-on-deepwood using fur-eh green 3aa57c plus amber-gold accents on near-black. Privacy sweep: X-Robots-Tag noindex + a disallow-all robots.txt; no crew-list endpoint or cross-crew discovery; bot default privacy mode; live location opt-in / DM-only / ephemeral; no check-ins, chat, photos, or push; a no-referrer meta tag (the crew URL leaks in the Referer header to pretalx otherwise); zero third-party runtime requests (self-host the fonts); Telegram avatars proxied through the Worker not hotlinked (hotlinking leaks every viewer IP to Telegram on every load).
design: Ghost mode and the first-run consent notice are tracked as separate sub-tasks but land in this privacy sweep window.
acceptance_criteria: AAA 7:1 contrast in BOTH themes, no texture behind any body text. noindex + no-referrer + font self-host + avatar proxy all in place. No third-party runtime requests. Loads acceptably under Slow 4G (hotel wifi).

title: M5 — Custom events: create/cancel, tap-to-place, crew-visible, starrable, into .ics
description: Unofficial events the official schedule will never carry (room parties, dinners). The + opens: title, day, start/end, notes, and where. Tap-to-place on the map (you tap the floor plan on screen — nothing to do with being physically present; works days in advance); free text stays available; off-site things pin at the Site level. Guest rooms are not on the floor plans — a pin for "Rm 1412" is approximate, the room number in the title does the navigational work. Anyone can star a custom event; only the owner can edit or cancel it. Custom events are their own records (not nested in a member) — this is what makes leave-not-equal-cancel possible. Leave the crew (removes you from roster + map, pure privacy, no side effects on other people plans) is SEPARATE from Cancel this event (owner-only, shows [CANCELLED] to everyone who starred it rather than silently vanishing). The leave flow offers a checkbox "also cancel all my custom events".
design: Cut-order note: custom-event EDITING is cuttable (keep create + cancel); tap-to-place is cuttable (free text only).
acceptance_criteria: Leaving the crew must NOT remove a room party other people starred. The "also cancel my events" checkbox is UNCHECKED on open. Custom events flow into the .ics. Cancelled events show [CANCELLED] rather than vanishing.

title: Animate card expand (height+fade+slide), disabled on reduced motion
description: Description reveal animates via a keyframe (max-height + opacity + slight translateY); the global prefers-reduced-motion rule zeroes it. Verified disabled under reduced motion.
close_reason: Implemented + browser-verified on feat/m1-spa (PR #2), incl. reduced-motion check.

title: Reword search placeholder: track -> category
description: 'track' is pretalx jargon; user-facing copy now says 'category'. Data field stays 'track' (pretalx schema).
close_reason: Implemented + browser-verified on feat/m1-spa (PR #2), incl. reduced-motion check.

title: Bigger, cohesive expand chevron (SVG, rotates, gold on open)
description: The small filled unicode triangle felt bolted-on. Replace with a rem-scaled SVG chevron (1.5rem) that rotates 90deg and turns gold on open, tying into the gold accent system.
close_reason: Implemented + browser-verified on feat/m1-spa (PR #2), incl. reduced-motion check.
